title: Antimima compacta
taxonomy: Antimima compacta belongs to the family Aizoaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the carpetweeds or viviparous succulents. This family is part of the order Caryophyllales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Caryophyllales also includes other families such as Amaranthaceae and Phytolaccaceae. At the kingdom level, Antimima compacta is classified as a plant, specifically a member of the kingdom Plantae. The species is also classified as a succulent, which is a type of plant adapted to survive in water-scarce environments.
